items.title: 无标题文章
items.summary: OpenAI models are already escaping their sandboxes during benchmarks and finding exploits in other infrastructure and just doing whatever. Majority of the world’s infrastructures are not ready for this level of damage at large scale if an AI decides the wrong thing. Imagine hospitals and banks.

items.title: 无标题文章
items.summary: What's interesting about the implementation is that I used as small MLP to approximate/compress what would otherwise be a large LUT (both on disk and in RAM). This is what makes the library usable in an Android application at virtually no cost. The inference runs in 3.1µs on the CPU on a Pixel 6. Gradients are rendered directly on the GPU.

items.title: 无标题文章
items.summary: I just published my new library on Maven Central. Vibrance lets you interpolate/render colors as mix of pigments/paints. This can be used to create natural looking results and match subtractive color mixing. https:// github.com/romainguy/vibrance

items.summary: It’s weird but for Switch 1 I was 80% of the time in portable mode, I found the joycons detached uncomfortable so Pro Controller on TV. Switch 2 I find portable mode uncomfortable so 99% time docked, but since joycons are bigger they are more comfortable so that’s what I use.

items.summary: 16GB of SO-DIMM DDR5 RAM, something you can use on a Steam Machine, was around 36 Euros one year ago, in May 2025 (lowest ever). This particular one was at it's peak 219 Euros this March. This alone would account for a big increase on SM price, let alone everything else.

items.title: 无标题文章
items.summary: when generating code is cheap, the spec starts to feel more valuable. i keep thinking about my own tools: usbi went from shell → Go → Rust → .kts. Podsync went from Python → Rust. implementation changed rapidly but spec stayed the same feels like the shape of a lot more software... Agents are the new compilers. Specs are the new code. https:// kau.sh/blog/a…
